title: Phase 5: Integration, companion protocol, polish
description: Companion protocol: auto-add macOS/iOS network as profile on connect. Per-network health indicator (last scan, device count, gateway reachability). Data separation: tag devices with network profile ID, per-network device cache. Edge cases: offline network stale data, same device on multiple networks, VPN subnet detection. Integration tests: scan local → switch manual remote → verify different device lists.

title: Enhanced ping UI with latency graph - both platforms

description:
Review and harden the enhanced ping implementation:
1. Verify iOS PingToolViewModel handles edge cases: 0 results, all timeouts, single result
2. Verify macOS inline-state version handles same edge cases
3. Ensure chartYAxisMax handles edge cases (empty, single point, all same value)
4. Ensure ping count Picker persists selection to UserDefaults correctly
5. Verify stop/cancel mid-ping leaves clean state (no zombie tasks)
6. Both platforms should show 'No results' or empty state when pings fail completely
7. Verify accessibility identifiers are set on chart, stats card, and count picker
title: Review and harden ping implementation
close_reason: Hardened: chartYAxisMax 10ms floor, pingCount UserDefaults persistence, Task.isCancelled guards, accessibility IDs on chart/stats

title: Unit tests for enhanced ping
close_reason: 46 new unit tests: iOS VM live stats/chartYAxisMax/successfulPings/lifecycle/persistence + macOS parser edge cases
description:
Write comprehensive unit tests for enhanced ping:
1. PingToolViewModel: test live stats computation (liveAvgLatency, liveMinLatency, liveMaxLatency)
2. PingToolViewModel: test chartYAxisMax with various distributions (normal, spike, single, empty)
3. PingToolViewModel: test successfulPings filter excludes timeouts
4. PingToolViewModel: test startPing/stopPing lifecycle with MockPingService that yields results
5. PingToolViewModel: test pingCount UserDefaults persistence
6. macOS: test ShellPingOutputParser with malformed input, empty output, partial output
7. Shared PingService: test calculateStatistics with edge cases
Target: 85%+ coverage on ping-related code. Use Swift Testing (@Test, #expect).

close_reason: 9 new UI tests: iOS chart/stats/picker/clear + macOS chart/stats/picker/close
description:
Write UI tests confirming the enhanced ping experience:
1. iOS: verify chart appears after 2+ successful pings (look for pingTool_chart_latency)
2. iOS: verify live stats (min/avg/max) appear during ping execution
3. iOS: verify ping count picker changes count and persists
4. iOS: verify stats card appears after ping completes
5. macOS: verify latencyChartView appears after 2+ results
6. macOS: verify stats line (min/avg/max) in footer after completion
7. macOS: verify count picker selection works
8. Both: verify clear button removes chart and stats
Use accessibility identifiers already in the views. Add any missing ones.

description: Implement multi-network scanning per docs/multi-network-scanning-plan.md. Covers both iOS and macOS. 5 phases: core model, scan strategy, iOS UI, macOS UI, integration.

description: Add ScanStrategy enum (.full, .remote) to NetworkScanKit. Extend ScanContext with networkProfile and scanStrategy fields. Add ScanPipeline.forStrategy() factory (.full = standard 4-step, .remote = TCP+ICMP+DNS only). Modify ScanEngine.scan() to accept strategy (backward compat, defaults to .full). Add IPv4Helpers.hostsInSubnet(cidr:) for remote subnet IP generation. Tests for pipeline strategy selection and CIDR parsing.
title: Phase 2: ScanStrategy + pipeline adaptation (NetworkScanKit)
